questions.question: Explain the difference between DoS and DDoS attacks with an example of each.
keyPoints: DoS originates from single source; DDoS from multiple distributed sources
DoS easier to block; DDoS harder due to distributed nature
Example: DoS - SYN Flood from one IP; DDoS - Botnet attack from thousands of zombies

questions.question: What is a botnet? How is it used in DDoS attacks?
keyPoints: Botnet is a network of compromised computers (zombies/bots)
Attacker uses command-and-control servers to control bots
Bots flood target simultaneously, creating massive traffic
Makes attack powerful and source difficult to trace

questions.question: Describe the working of a SYN Flood attack. Why is it effective?
keyPoints: Exploits TCP three-way handshake
Attacker sends SYN requests but never completes handshake
Creates half-open connections that consume server resources
Effective because servers allocate memory for pending connections
Difficult to distinguish from legitimate connection surges

questions.question: What are amplification attacks? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Attacker sends small requests to vulnerable servers
Servers send much larger responses to victim (amplification factor)
Victim overwhelmed by traffic they didn't request
Example: DNS amplification - small DNS query triggers large DNS response
NTP amplification also uses same principle

questions.question: List three symptoms of a DDoS attack and three methods to mitigate DDoS attacks.
keyPoints: Symptoms: Slow network performance, website inaccessibility, sudden spam, traffic from unusual locations
Mitigation: CDN distribution, rate limiting, traffic scrubbing, firewalls, IDS/IPS

questions.question: Explain the Ping of Death attack and how it works.
keyPoints: Sends ICMP packets larger than maximum allowed size (65,535 bytes)
Causes buffer overflow in target system
Can crash or freeze the target computer
Exploits vulnerability in how systems handle oversized packets

questions.question: What are Application Layer (Layer 7) DDoS attacks? How do they differ from network layer attacks?
keyPoints: Target specific applications/web servers
Exhaust server resources with legitimate-looking requests
Harder to detect than network layer attacks
Network layer attacks overwhelm bandwidth; application layer attacks overwhelm server processing
Examples: HTTP flood, Slowloris, RUDY

questions.question: Describe different types of DDoS attacks. Classify them into volumetric, protocol, and application layer attacks with examples of each.
keyPoints: Volumetric/Volume-based: UDP flood, ICMP flood - saturate bandwidth
Protocol attacks: SYN flood, Ping of Death, Smurf - exploit network protocols
Application layer: HTTP flood, Slowloris, RUDY - target web applications
Explain working of each with example
Current trend is toward hybrid and multi-vector attacks

questions.question: Explain the working of a Smurf attack. How does IP spoofing help in this attack? Also suggest countermeasures against Smurf attacks.
keyPoints: Smurf uses ICMP echo request to broadcast address
Attacker spoofs source IP as victim's IP
All hosts on network reply to victim, overwhelming it
Countermeasures: Disable IP directed broadcast on routers, configure firewalls to block ICMP, use anti-spoofing filters

questions.question: Discuss various DDoS mitigation techniques and strategies that organizations can implement to protect their infrastructure.
keyPoints: CDN for distributing traffic
Rate limiting to control request rates
Traffic scrubbing to filter malicious packets
Firewalls and IDS/IPS
Blackhole routing
Anycast for distributing load
Redundant infrastructure
DDoS protection services (Cloudflare, Akamai)
Incident response planning

questions.question: Explain what is meant by 'Reflection Attack' in DDoS. How does it differ from 'Amplification Attack'? Explain with examples.
keyPoints: Reflection: Attacker sends requests with spoofed victim IP to third parties, causing responses to go to victim
Amplification: Reflection attacks where response is much larger than request
DNS amplification uses DNS servers
NTP amplification uses NTP servers
Both use victim as destination but amplification has size multiplier
